LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS THE WRIT AS TO THE 33 PERMIT APPLICATIONS THAT WERE DENIED BECAUSE ALTERNATIVE SITES EXISTED. THE COURT DENIES THE PETITION AS TO THE 6 PERMIT APPLICATIONS THAT WERE DENIED BASED UPON THE FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ARTICLE 27. PARTIES SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION TO ENFORCE WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. RESPONDENTS PROVIDE NO AUTHORITY THAT THE SUBSTITUTION OF A SMALLER AND QUIETER SMF AT THE 1157 CHURCH STREET LOCATION THAT RESPONDENTS' OWN DECLARANT ACKNOWLEDGES WAS APPROVED BY THE DIRECTOR OF PUBLIC WORKS AND IS CONSISTENT WITH THE WRIT OF MANDATE ORDER AND WOULD BENEFIT THE APPLICABLE COMMUNITIES (FONG DEC. PARA. 7) RE-STARTS THE ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS PROCESS. NOTHING IN THE PRIOR ORDER CONTEMPLATES THAT SUCH A SUBSTITUTION WOULD REQUIRE ANOTHER APPEALS PROCESS, NOR WOULD IT HAVE BEEN CONSISTENT WITH THE PRIOR ORDER TO HAVE SUCH A PROVISION IN IT. EVEN IF THE APPEALS PROCESS WAS RE-STARTED BY THE ISSUANCE OF THE PERMIT ON JULY 21, 2016, THE TIME TO APPEAL EXPIRED PRIOR TO THE APPEAL FILED BY MS. TONGUE ON OCTOBER 3. RESPONDENTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE LIMITATION PERIOD ON THE APPEAL WAS DELAYED OR TOLLED BECAUSE OF LACK OF A REQUIRED NOTICE BY PETITIONER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT WITH ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE ADDED (SEE ORDER FOR FULL TEXT). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-07-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Donald Falk and Elspeth Hansen of Mayer, Brown, Rowe & Maw LLP telephone number 650-331-2000 for Petitioner Pacific Bell Telephone Company.
William Sanders of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-6771 for Respondents City and County of San Francisco, et al.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed it tentative ruling.
Petitioner Pacific Bell Telephone Company's motion to enforce writ of mandate is granted. Respondents provide no authority that the substitution of a smaller and quieter SMF at the 1157 Church Street location that respondents' own declarant acknowledges was approved by the Director of Public Works and is consistent with the writ of mandate order and would benefit the applicable communities (Fong Dec. para. 7) re-starts the administrative appeals process. Nothing in the prior order contemplates that such a substitution would require another appeals process, nor would it have been consistent with the prior order to have such a provision in it. Even if the appeals process was re-started by the issuance of the permit on July 21, 2016, the time to appeal expired prior to the appeal filed by Ms. Tongue on October 3. Respondents have not shown that the limitation period on the appeal was delayed or tolled because of lack of a required notice by petitioner. Order signed in open Court with additional language added (see order for full text).

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or, In The Alternative, Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Donald M. Falk, of Mayer Brown LLP, Tel. 650.331.2000, for petitioners;
William K. Sanders, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4700, for respondents.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY's Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is granted and denied in part. The court grants the writ as to the 33 permit applications that were denied because alternative sites existed. "It is well settled an administrative agency is bound by its own rules and regulations." Talmo v. Civil Service Com. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 210, 218. The SMF Order is such a regulation. It is titled "Regulations for Issuing Excavation Permits for the installation of Surface-Mounted Facilities in the Public Rights-Of-Way." Petitioner correctly explains how the hearing process is supposed to work. (AR 2134). Petitioner had no obligation to propose multiple sites at the outset of the process. If other locations are proposed at the hearing, then the hearing officer has the discretion to keep the hearing open for an additional 20 days and entertain supplemental evidence. The hearing officers did not take advantage of this provision, however. The officers merely denied the applications and stated that other less intrusive alternatives were available. Respondent erred. When the petitioner's application complied with the other sections of the SMF Order (as they did in this case), the hearing officer and director had the authority to either approve the site or approve the site with changes. The MOU did not change this process. Respondent enacted Article 27 to insure that neighbors near the alternative sites would receive notice.

The court denies the petition as to the 6 permit applications that were denied based upon the failure to comply with Article 27. Pursuant to Gov't 65922(b), the Permit Streamlining Act does not apply to administrative appeals within a state or local agency or to a state or local agency. Therefore, the "prospectively only" language of Cal. Gov't Code sec. 65942 does not apply to this case. Respondent did not violate Cal. Public Utilities Code sec. 5885(c)(2) because that provision applies to a completed application for an "encroachment permit" and this case involves the pre-application process for excavation permits. Lastly, petitioner does not establish that it was singled out. Article 27 applies to all SMF applicants including PG&E and Comcast. Parties shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
